module_4

scrape

src.scrape.load_data(fileName)

Load and deserialise a JSON file from disk.

Opens the file at fileName in read mode and parses its contents using json.load(), returning the deserialised Python object. The file is automatically closed when the with block exits, whether or not an exception occurs.

Parameters:

fileName (str or os.PathLike) – Path to the JSON file to load. Accepts both absolute and relative paths.

Returns:

The deserialised contents of the JSON file. The return type mirrors the top-level JSON structure — typically a dict or list.

Return type:

dict or list

Raises:

  • FileNotFoundError – If no file exists at fileName.

  • PermissionError – If the process does not have read permission for the file.

  • json.JSONDecodeError – If the file contents are not valid JSON.

  • IsADirectoryError – If fileName resolves to a directory rather than a file.

src.scrape.scrape_data(lastIDFetched)

Scrape and parse graduate application entries from GradCafe, stopping when previously fetched records are reached or pages are exhausted.

Iterates through paginated GradCafe survey results page by page, fetching each via _fetchPage(), parsing the HTML table body with BeautifulSoup, and cleaning each page’s rows via clean.clean_data(). Accumulates cleaned records across pages until one of the following termination conditions is met:

Termination conditions:

Condition

Behaviour

HTTP status is not 200

Returns None

clean.clean_data() returns None (lastIDFetched was encountered)

Stops pagination; returns accumulated data

A page yields fewer than 20 records (final or near-final page reached)

Stops pagination; returns accumulated data

All pages exhausted without hitting any other termination condition

Returns accumulated data
Parameters:

lastIDFetched (int or None) – The integer ID of the most recently scraped record from a prior run. Passed to clean.clean_data() on each page to detect where new data ends and already-seen data begins. Pass 0 or None to scrape all available records.

Returns:

A flat list of cleaned application record dictionaries accumulated across all pages, or None if any page fetch returned a non-200 HTTP status. Returns an empty list if no new records were found before the last page.

Return type:

list[dict] or None

Raises:

  • AttributeError – If the parsed HTML page does not contain a <tbody> element, causing tableBody.find_all to be called on None.

  • Exception – Any unhandled exception raised by _fetchPage() or clean.clean_data(), such as network errors or malformed row structure, will propagate to the caller uncaught.

Note

Each call to _fetchPage() incurs a random 0–5 second polite delay. For a dataset spanning n pages, expect a minimum wall-clock time of approximately n * 0 seconds and a maximum of n * 5 seconds, plus network latency.

Note

The < 20 records heuristic for detecting the final page assumes GradCafe serves exactly 20 entries per full page. If the source site changes its page size, this condition may terminate scraping prematurely or fail to detect the last page.

Warning

If tableBody is None (e.g. GradCafe changes its HTML structure or returns a CAPTCHA or error page with a 200 status), tableBody.find_all("tr") will raise an AttributeError and scraping will abort without returning partial data. Consider adding a None guard on tableBody before proceeding.

See also

_fetchPage() — fetches and returns raw page content for a given page number.

clean.clean_data() — parses raw <tr> elements into cleaned record dictionaries and detects the lastIDFetched boundary.
